topic: LLM Prompt Caching Guide: OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini in 2026

competitors.title: LLM Prompt Caching: Cut API Costs by 90% (All 3 Providers)
key_points: Comprehensive side-by-side comparison of OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini with identical Python SDK code examples for all three providers

Detailed provider comparison table: OpenAI (automatic, 50% discount, 5-10min TTL), Anthropic (automatic + explicit, 90% discount, 5min/1hr TTL), Gemini (implicit + explicit, ~90% discount, configurable TTL)

Production cost calculator with 3 real scenarios: Hobby (2K system prompt, 100 req/day → $12.60/mo savings on Anthropic), Growth (8K prefix, 10K req/day → $576/mo savings), Enterprise (10K prefix, 100K req/day → $7,200/mo or $86,400/yr savings)
Anti-patterns section: timestamps in system prompts, user content before static content, rotating few-shot examples, dynamic tool definitions, prompts below minimum token threshold
Monitor cache hit rate: healthy production systems should sustain 70-90% hit rates; below 50% indicates prompt structure problems
Covers Anthropic February 2026 automatic caching update and Gemini 2.5 implicit caching

competitors.title: Anthropic Prompt Caching in 2026: Cost, TTL, and Latency Planning
key_points: Operational focus: caching is production infrastructure, not a developer convenience — should be modeled like batching and concurrency controls
5-minute vs 1-hour TTL decision framework based on request cadence, not philosophy
Common failure mode: placing cache breakpoints after changing content breaks prefix matching and wastes cache write spend
Automatic vs explicit breakpoint guidance: start simple, measure, only add complexity when simpler patterns leave savings on the table
Latency matters beyond cost: well-designed cache improves time-to-first-token and reduces timeout complaints in interactive apps

competitors.title: Prompt Caching: Save 50-90% on LLM API Costs [2026 Guide]
key_points: 31% of LLM queries exhibit semantic similarity — representing massive inefficiency without caching
Applications with knowledge bases or lengthy instructions see 60-80% cost reduction through prompt caching
Provider comparison: OpenAI (automatic, 50% savings), Anthropic (manual cache_control, 90% savings), Gemini (automatic, 50% savings)
OpenAI GPT-5.1 series offers 24-hour cache retention for consistent system prompts
Anthropic pricing detail: Claude Sonnet 4.5 cache write $3.75/1M, cache read $0.30/1M (90% savings)
Best practices: static content first, meet minimum 1,024 token threshold, maintain exact string consistency, monitor cache hit rates targeting 70%+

competitors.title: Anthropic Prompt Caching Deep Dive — Automatic Caching, 1-Hour TTL, and the Gotchas
key_points: Real production incident: $13.86 daily bill caused by library update silently changing cache TTL from 1-hour to 5-minutes
February 2026 automatic caching update: single top-level cache_control replaces per-block marking; system auto-places breakpoint on last cacheable block
Auto-advancing in multi-turn conversations: cache breakpoint moves forward automatically without manual cache_control updates
1-hour TTL pricing: Opus 4.6 example — base $5/MTok, 5-min write $6.25 (1.25x), 1-hour write $10 (2x), cache read $0.50 (0.1x) — 60% more on writes for 12x the TTL
4 breakpoint slot limit: automatic caching uses 1 of 4 slots; adding auto + 4 explicit = 400 error
Cache invalidation hierarchy: tools → system → messages (least-changed at bottom, most-changed on top); changing anything below invalidates all caches above
20-block lookback window limitation in long conversations

stats.fact: Prompt caching reduces LLM API costs by 50-90% and latency by up to 85%
Anthropic cache reads cost 0.1x base input price (90% discount); OpenAI offers 50% discount; Gemini offers ~90% discount
31% of LLM queries exhibit semantic similarity — representing massive inefficiency without caching
Applications with knowledge bases or lengthy instructions see 60-80% cost reduction through prompt caching
Enterprise scenario: 100K requests/day with 10K token cached prefix saves $7,200/month ($86,400/year) on Anthropic
Anthropic automatic caching (Feb 2026) eliminates per-block cache_control marking — single top-level cache_control auto-places breakpoint
Anthropic 1-hour TTL costs 2x base input price on writes vs 1.25x for 5-minute TTL; cache reads are 0.1x for both — pays for itself after 2 reads
OpenAI prompt caching minimum threshold: 1,024 tokens with 128-token increment matching; GPT-5.1 offers 24-hour cache retention
Anthropic provides up to 4 cache breakpoints per prompt; automatic caching uses 1 of 4 slots
Hugging Face technical analysis measured ~5.21x speedup on T4 GPUs from KV cache reuse
Healthy production systems should sustain 70-90% cache hit rates; below 50% indicates prompt structure problems
Growth scenario: 10K requests/day with 8K cached prefix saves $576/month on Anthropic despite higher base price than OpenAI

angles: Zero-config savings vs maximum control: OpenAI's automatic caching requires no code changes (50% discount), while Anthropic's explicit breakpoints offer 90% savings with fine-grained control — comparing the tradeoff
The $13.86/day lesson: Real production incidents where caching misconfigurations caused bill explosions, and how TTL selection (5-min vs 1-hour) is an operational decision, not a philosophical one

Automatic caching changes everything (Feb 2026): Anthropic's shift from per-block cache_control to single top-level auto-placement reduces implementation complexity and eliminates the most common misconfiguration patterns

Prompt structure as infrastructure: Why prompt ordering (tools → system → context → user query) isn't just good practice — it directly determines your cache hit rate and API bill, with concrete anti-patterns and fixes
Beyond cost: How caching improves time-to-first-token, reduces timeout complaints in interactive apps, and enables more predictable throughput for production systems

Multi-provider strategy: When to use each provider's caching (OpenAI for simplicity, Anthropic for cost-sensitive high-volume, Gemini for configurable TTL) — with production cost calculator showing real dollar amounts
